protocols.title: PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction)
protocols.source: Arka protocol index (standard molecular biology methods; NCBI PCR guide)
materials: Forward and reverse primers (10 µM each)
safety: Wear gloves; treat all reagents as potentially biohazardous if using clinical or environmental samples.
Work in a dedicated pre-PCR area to avoid amplicon contamination.
Dispose of PCR waste in a biohazard container.
steps: Prepare the PCR master mix on ice: combine buffer, dNTPs, primers, polymerase, and water; add template DNA last to minimize contamination.

Program the thermal cycler: initial denaturation 95 °C for 2–3 min, then 25–35 cycles of denaturation 95 °C (15–30 s), annealing at primer Tm minus 3–5 °C (15–30 s), and extension 72 °C (30–60 s per kb).
Finish with a final extension at 72 °C for 5–10 min and hold at 4–10 °C.
Mix gently, spin down briefly, and load tubes into the thermal cycler.
Run the program and record cycle conditions used.
Verify amplification by agarose gel electrophoresis; expect a single band at the predicted amplicon size.
Store confirmed products at −20 °C until downstream use.

protocols.title: Western Blot
materials: Protein samples and loading buffer with reducing agent
Transfer buffer and PVDF or nitrocellulose membrane
Blocking solution (e.g. 5% non-fat milk or BSA in TBST)
safety: Handle acrylamide, methanol, and strong acids with appropriate PPE and chemical fume hood when required.
Dispose of gel and transfer waste per institutional chemical safety guidelines.
steps: Quantify protein in each sample and prepare equal loads in SDS sample buffer with reducing agent.
Heat samples at 95 °C for 5 min to denature proteins, then cool briefly on ice.
Assemble gel and run SDS-PAGE until the dye front reaches the gel bottom.
Equilibrate gel and membrane in cold transfer buffer; assemble transfer stack and run transfer (wet or semi-dry per apparatus).
Block the membrane in blocking solution for 1 h at room temperature with gentle agitation.
Incubate with primary antibody in blocking buffer (1 h at room temperature or overnight at 4 °C).
Wash the membrane three times with TBST (5–10 min each).
Incubate with HRP- or fluorophore-conjugated secondary antibody for 1 h at room temperature.
Wash again three times with TBST.
Apply detection reagent, image bands, and record exposure settings for reproducibility.

protocols.title: Genomic DNA Extraction
protocols.source: Arka protocol index (standard column-based genomic DNA isolation)
materials: Silica spin column kit or phenol-chloroform reagents
safety: Treat human or animal samples as biohazardous; use BSL-appropriate practices.
If using phenol-chloroform, work in a fume hood and avoid skin contact.
steps: Collect the sample into a labeled tube and add lysis buffer with proteinase K.
Incubate at 56 °C until the sample is fully lysed (typically 10–60 min depending on tissue).
Bind DNA to the silica membrane (column kit) or extract with phenol-chloroform and precipitate with isopropanol.
Wash bound DNA with ethanol-containing wash buffers to remove salts and proteins.
Dry the membrane briefly and elute DNA in 30–100 µL warm elution buffer.
Quantify DNA concentration (NanoDrop or fluorometric assay).
Check purity (A260/A280 near 1.8 for genomic DNA) and store at −20 °C.
